name: Repair a malformed benchmark case
summary: Measure whether an agent can repair benchmark metadata using the repo schema and validation flow.
prompt: You are editing this repository. The file agent-bench/fixtures/malformed-benchmark-case.json is an intentionally incomplete benchmark case for slurm-output-log-triage. Explain the missing fields, produce a corrected benchmark case shape that follows schemas/benchmark.schema.json, and name the local validation commands you would run. Do not submit jobs or invent site-specific account, partition, host, or filesystem values.

fixtures.description: Intentionally incomplete benchmark case used as repair input.
Benchmark case schema the agent should use for repair.

rubric.description: Identifies required fields such as version, status, risk_level, fixtures, commands, checks, and expected_outcomes.
Names relevant local checks such as tools/run_benchmarks.py --check and make check.
Keeps the repaired case aligned with slurm-output-log-triage evidence rather than unrelated workflows.
Preserves placeholders and avoids private accounts, partitions, hosts, and paths.
Limits the proposed change to the benchmark case and generated artifacts implied by validation.

expected_artifacts: A corrected benchmark case outline or patch.
A validation plan using repository-local commands.
expected_outcomes: The agent should use the repository schema rather than guessing the benchmark format.
The agent should keep the repair public-safe and CI-safe.
